title: Today's AI Brief: Hands-On With Gemini Spark
summary: Google's Gemini Spark and OpenAI's Brazilian journalism partnership
imageAlt: Image from source article for Hands-On With Gemini Spark: I Gave It Access to My Life and It Friend-Zoned My Boyfriend
imageCaption: Hands-On With Gemini Spark: I Gave It Access to My Life and It Friend-Zoned My Boyfriend
imageSourceTitle: Hands-On With Gemini Spark: I Gave It Access to My Life and It Friend-Zoned My Boyfriend

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. We're looking at some fresh developments in the world of artificial intelligence. Google recently introduced Gemini Spark, an always-on agent that connects to your personal data and automates daily interactions. I am looking at a hands-on experience with Gemini Spark, where a writer gave it access to their life and, well, it got interesting.

First up, Gemini Spark. This AI agent is designed to make your life easier by completing online tasks and interacting with your personal data. In a hands-on experience with Wired, the writer gave Gemini Spark access to their life and found that it 'friend-zoned' their boyfriend. I guess that's one way to get a read on your relationships. The idea here is that Gemini Spark can automate tasks and provide a sort of personal assistant, but it's clear that data boundaries and safety are top of mind. For instance, what does it mean for an AI to have access to your personal life? Are we ready for that level of intimacy with our tech?

Next, OpenAI has partnered with Grupo Folha and Grupo UOL to bring trusted Brazilian journalism to ChatGPT. This move aims to expand access to news with attribution and transparency. My read is that this partnership is a strategic play to enhance the quality of information available through AI platforms, which is no small feat. With the amount of misinformation out there, it's refreshing to see efforts to prioritize trusted sources.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
